children.heading: Rule of construction regarding certain civil rights laws
children.label: 1 Rule of construction regarding certain civil rights laws
children.text: For the purpose of applying the prohibitions against discrimination on the basis of age under the Age Discrimination Act of 1975 [ et seq.], on the basis of handicap under section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 [], on the basis of sex under title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 [ et seq.], or on the basis of race, color, or national origin under title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 [ et seq.], programs and activities funded in whole or in part with funds made available under section 300x or 300x–21 of this title shall be considered to be programs and activities receiving Federal financial assistance.42 U.S.C. 610129 U.S.C. 79420 U.S.C. 168142 U.S.C. 2000d

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 2 Prohibition
children.text: No person shall on the ground of sex (including, in the case of a woman, on the ground that the woman is pregnant), or on the ground of religion, be excluded from participation in, be denied the benefits of, or be subjected to discrimination under, any program or activity funded in whole or in part with funds made available under section 300x or 300x–21 of this title.

children.heading: Referrals to Attorney General after notice
children.label: 1 Referrals to Attorney General after notice
A
children.text: refer the matter to the Attorney General with a recommendation that an appropriate civil action be instituted;

children.label: B
children.text: exercise the powers and functions provided by the Age Discrimination Act of 1975 [ et seq.], section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 [], title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 [ et seq.], or title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 [ et seq.], as may be applicable; or42 U.S.C. 610129 U.S.C. 79420 U.S.C. 168142 U.S.C. 2000d

children.label: C
children.text: take such other actions as may be authorized by law.

children.heading: Authority of Attorney General
children.label: 2 Authority of Attorney General
children.text: When a matter is referred to the Attorney General pursuant to paragraph (1)(A), or whenever the Attorney General has reason to believe that a State or an entity is engaged in a pattern or practice in violation of a provision of law referred to in subsection (a)(1) or in violation of subsection (a)(2), the Attorney General may bring a civil action in any appropriate district court of the United States for such relief as may be appropriate, including injunctive relief.
